DECREES.Title: The Vigesima: Land Lease Taxation
DECREES.Description: All land leases in Venice are subject to a 20% tax known as the Vigesima (literally "twentieth"). This tax is automatically collected by the Republic on all lease income, reducing landowners' net revenue to 80% of the gross lease amount.
DECREES.Rationale: To fund essential public services and infrastructure, including canal maintenance, bridge repairs, and public security, while ensuring that the Republic benefits from private land development.
DECREES.FlavorText: "As the waters of the lagoon sustain our city, so too must the wealth of our lands sustain the Republic." - Doge Francesco Foscari
DECREES.HistoricalInspiration: Based on various property taxes in Renaissance Venice, including the Decima (tenth) which was a 10% property tax instituted in 1463 to fund naval wars against the Ottoman Empire.
DECREES.Notes: This decree is implemented in the distributeLeases.py script, which automatically collects 20% of all lease payments and transfers them to the ConsiglioDeiDieci treasury.
